sanskrit:
अक्षराणाम् अकारोऽस्मि द्वन्द्वः सामासिकस्य च।
अहमेवाक्षयः कालो धाताऽहं विश्वतोमुखः॥
transliteration: akṣarāṇām akāro ’smi dvandvaḥ sāmāsikasya ca | aham evākṣayaḥ kālo dhātāhaṁ viśvato-mukhaḥ ||
literal_meaning: Of letters, I am the letter A; of compounds, the dual; I am the inexhaustible time; I am the creator Brahmā of manifold faces.
contextual_meaning: Krishna names the foundational units of language, time, and creativity as his own presence, reminding Arjuna that every beginning, every pair, every ending, and every creative impulse is an expression of the same conscious source.
psychological_state: Cognitive expansion—shifting from fragmented self-references to perceiving oneself as the alphabet, the sentence, the clock, and the author all at once.
trigger_scenario: Staring at a blinking cursor on a blank document, realizing the letter 'A' you just typed is both trivial and the seed of every story you will ever write.
anchor_text: Every letter, moment, and face is the same conscious source expressing itself.
